title: At Olivia's Gate
subtitle: Twelfth Night · the persuasion task. SCHEMA ONLY — content stubbed (design deliverable, not build).
adjudicatorOverlay: STUB. Shakespeare stages you as Viola ('Cesario') at Olivia's gate; he plays Olivia's resistance and, at the close, steps out to compare your attempt to the play's own solution.

positionOptions.label: STUB — enter as Cesario with a persuasive aim

theories.label: STUB — your reading of what will move Olivia

phases.label: State Your Aim
phases.instruction: STUB — commit up front to what you intend to make Olivia feel, and why the text says it could work.

phases.label: The Gate
phases.instruction: STUB — Olivia resists within textual limits. Misreading the target gets the door shut with the very line that shuts it (e.g. 1.5).

phases.label: Your Suit
phases.instruction: STUB — cite the play's own rhetoric you are drawing on; each is ruled for how the text licenses it.

phases.label: Compared to the Play
phases.instruction: STUB — your attempt is set beside Viola's own solution; what did the willow-cabin speech do that yours did not.

resistanceAgents.label: STUB — Olivia
resistanceAgents.constraints: STUB — resists within the text; shuts the door with a canonical line on a misread.

admissibilityRubric: STUB — a suit is inadmissible if it assumes intimacy or knowledge the text does not grant Cesario at this point in the plot.
evidenceRubric: STUB — rule each rhetorical move: supports / consistent-with / contradicts the persuasive aim, tied to the text.
closingTemplate: STUB — compare the user's attempt to the play's solution; name what the willow-cabin speech achieved; unused-evidence retrieval over the scene's rhetoric.
